title: Cleaning for Pesach: The Complete Chametz Removal Guide

sources.title: Where To Search - Laws of Bedikat Chametz
key_points: Search by candle light on night of 14th Nissan
Check all rooms where chametz might have been brought
High holes (beyond reach) and low holes (under 3 handbreadths) don't require checking
Synagogues and study halls require checking because children bring chametz
One should check pockets and clothing
Ten pieces of chametz customarily hidden for the search

sources.title: Shulchan Aruch, Orach Chayim 433 - Laws of Checking for Chametz
key_points: 11 paragraphs of detailed laws
Search must be by candlelight, not moonlight or sunlight
Use a single candle, not a torch
Check all places where chametz may have been brought
Wine cellars and barns don't require checking
Cattle sheds and chicken coops don't need checking - animals would eat chametz
Clean rooms before the 13th of Nissan still require checking on 14th
Check pockets and sleeves where chametz may be kept

sources.title: What Is Passover (Pesach)? - Chabad.org
key_points: Passover commemorates the Exodus from Egypt
Chametz (leavened bread) strictly avoided during 8 days
Search for chametz on night before Passover
Burning of chametz the next morning
Selling chametz to non-Jew is common practice
Bedikat chametz involves blessing, candle, feather, wooden spoon
